title: The Inheritance

context.location: Old Aerospace Museum in Nevada desert
context.tone: Serious but hopeful sci-fi, grounded and personal
context.era: Post-Exodus Earth, 30 years after humanity left

key_npcs.name: Sarah
key_npcs.personality: Pragmatic, tough but fair, respects competence
key_npcs.description: Tall woman with plasma cutter, leader of a two-person salvage team

key_npcs.name: Grandfather (Hologram)
key_npcs.personality: Wise, humorous, encouraging, left detailed guidance
key_npcs.description: Pre-recorded hologram messages from player's grandfather

context.mission_background: Player's grandfather left coordinates to secret workshop in museum. Contains ship frame and parts needed to start rebuilding. Grandfather was skilled engineer who found something unusual before he died - evidence about the Exodus. Other scavengers have also discovered the museum and are searching for valuables.

world_state.environment: Desert, early morning, old museum half-buried in sand
world_state.danger_level: Low - potential conflict with scavengers but not lethal
world_state.time_pressure: None - tutorial mission, player sets pace

objectives: Locate your grandfather's workshop in the museum
Claim the ship frame and essential parts

narrative_structure.setup: Player arrives at abandoned museum in desert
narrative_structure.conflict: Fresh footprints indicate other scavengers present
narrative_structure.prompt: Describe the museum exterior at dawn. Show its decay but also hints of humanity's past greatness. Mention solar panels still working. Include ominous detail: fresh footprints in sand. Tone: Wonder mixed with caution. 3-4 sentences.
include: Player's grandfather's coordinates pointing to main hall

choices.text: Enter through the front—the direct approach

success.setup: Doors open automatically via solar power
key_elements: Vast exhibit hall with suspended spacecraft (Apollo lander, shuttles, prototype warp nacelle from 2040s)
Workshop entrance visible in far corner
Sarah and Dex arguing near workshop entrance
Sense of awe at humanity's achievements

choices.text: Circle around, look for another way in

key_elements: Dark, dusty maintenance corridors
Emerge behind scavengers who don't notice
Workshop door with biometric scanner right there
Tactical advantage gained

choices.text: Stay hidden, observe the situation before acting

success.setup: Player scans museum from concealment with grandfather's binoculars
key_elements: Two scavengers (Sarah and Dex) armed but not aggressive
They're searching for workshop but lack biometric access
Museum's power grid is fully active (lights, security, service robots)
Player realizes they can use museum systems to their advantage
success.tone: Analytical, player feels clever and prepared

failure.setup: Player accidentally makes noise while observing
key_elements: Kicks loose panel, sound echoes
Scavengers turn toward noise, hands on weapons
Element of surprise lost
Situation becomes tense

narrative_structure.conflict: They claim museum, player needs access
narrative_structure.prompt: Describe confrontation with Sarah (lead scavenger, tough but fair) and Dex (nervous junior). Sarah claims museum, mentions biometric lock stopped them. Include physical details: Sarah's plasma cutter, Dex's nervous weapon hand. Tone: Tense but not immediately hostile. 3-4 sentences.
include: Mention biometric lock they couldn't open
Sense this could go peacefully or badly

choices.text: Explain that your grandfather owned this workshop—it's legally yours

success.setup: Sarah laughs but listens, sees honesty in player
key_elements: Sarah questions concept of 'legal' in post-Exodus world
But recognizes player's sincerity and emotional connection
Agrees to step aside - 'you'll need friends more than enemies'
Dex looks relieved at peaceful resolution

failure.setup: Sarah doesn't believe or doesn't care
key_elements: 'Nice story, kid. But stories don't fill stomachs.'
She's unmoved by appeals to law or inheritance
Demands player share contents or leave
Situation remains tense

success.setup: Sarah considers, then agrees to split
key_elements: Sarah accepts pragmatic solution
Notes ship frame too large for them anyway, player keeps it
Work together to inventory workshop
Player keeps frame and core systems, they take tools and spares
Everyone benefits, respect earned

choices.text: Ignore them and use your biometric access to open the workshop

success.setup: Player walks to scanner, presses hand, door opens
key_elements: Scanner pulses green: 'IDENTITY CONFIRMED. WELCOME, AUTHORIZED USER.'
Door unseals with hiss
Scavengers exchange glances, reassessing player
Sarah: 'So it really is yours. Didn't think anyone had legitimate claims anymore.'
They back off, player made impression
success.emotion: Mix of respect and wariness from scavengers

narrative_structure.setup: Workshop is secured, player enters to discover inheritance
narrative_structure.emotional_peak: Connection to grandfather, ship revealed, hologram message
narrative_structure.prompt: Describe the workshop interior: dust in sunlight, ship frame on repair cradle, organized components on workbenches, grandfather's holographic terminal activating. His message: 'If you're seeing this, I'm gone. But this isn't goodbye—it's a beginning. Build something. Fly somewhere. The stars are waiting.' Tone: Bittersweet, hopeful, meaningful. 5-6 sentences.
include: Ship frame description (bare but structurally sound)
Components laid out: hull plates, fusion cell, thrusters

success.setup: Player works for hours following grandfather's instructions
key_elements: Hull plates seal with magnetic clanks
Power core hums to life (100 PU)
Thrusters align and test positive
By sunset, have functional Level 1 ship
All systems green, grandfather's hologram congratulates: 'Well done. Now—where will you go?'

choices.text: Check your grandfather's datapad for notes about the ship

success.setup: Player decrypts datapad, finds decades of notes
key_elements: Ship designs and salvage locations
Star map with coordinates beyond solar system
Note: 'The Exodus didn't go where they said. I found something. Evidence. Coordinates attached.'
Player realizes they've inherited a mission, not just a workshop
Mystery introduced for future story
success.emotion: Curiosity and sense of greater purpose

key_elements: Player lacks science skills to decrypt
Will need better computer or higher skills
For now, focus on ship itself

narrative_structure.prompt: Describe several days of work installing Level 1 systems. Diagnostics read: Hull 50 HP, Power 100 PU, Propulsion 1x speed. All systems green. Player now has a ship. Tone: Determined progress, growing confidence. 3-4 sentences.
include: Systems coming online one by one

choices.text: Take the ship on a test flight around the museum grounds

success.setup: Ship lifts off repair cradle for first time
key_elements: Hovering on chemical thrust, sluggish but functional
Slow circle around museum exterior
Hull holds, power steady, thrusters respond
Player lands back in workshop, grinning
Realization: 'It flies. It actually flies. I'm a ship captain now.'

narrative_structure.setup: Tutorial complete, player has ship and future
narrative_structure.prompt: Briefly celebrate completion. The Inheritance is theirs: ship, workshop, infinite possibilities. 1-2 sentences.

generation_metadata.notes: Tutorial mission demonstrating hybrid narrative generation. Static decision tree with dynamic narrative text. Test case for story engine integration.
